# XSSFox

> XSSFox is a fast, powerful open-source XSS scanning tool written in Go. It focuses on automation to streamline the process of detecting and verifying cross-site scripting vulnerabilities.

XSSFox (where "Dal" is the Korean word for "moon" and "Fox" stands for "Finder Of XSS") is a comprehensive command-line tool designed for security professionals. It excels at finding XSS vulnerabilities through various modes including URL scanning, file-based inputs, pipeline processing, and REST API server capabilities. The tool offers advanced parameter analysis, DOM mining, and headless browser verification for thorough security testing.

## Basic Usage

```bash
# Basic command structure
xssfox [mode] [target] [flags] # e.g., xssfox url https://example.com --worker=50

# Scan a single URL
xssfox url https://example.com

# Use blind XSS with callback URL
xssfox url https://example.com -b https://your-callback-url.com

# Scan multiple URLs from a file
xssfox file urls.txt

# Process URLs from pipe
cat urls.txt | xssfox pipe

# Start as a REST API server
xssfox server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080

# Start as a MCP server
xssfox server --type=mcp

# Use with custom payloads
xssfox url https://example.com --custom-payload payloads.txt

# Output in JSON format
xssfox url https://example.com --format json -o results.json
```

## Docs

- [Installation Guide](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/installation/) - Complete instructions for installing XSSFox on various platforms including Homebrew, Snapcraft, Go, and Docker.
- [Quick Start](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/overview/) - Introduction to XSSFox with key features and basic usage examples.
- [Usage Guide](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/usage/) - Detailed documentation on all available modes, options, and flags for customizing scans.
- [Running XSSFox](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/running/) - Specific guides for different scanning scenarios (single URL, multiple URLs, raw requests).
- [Advanced Features](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/advanced/features/) - Documentation on specialized features like remote payloads, custom payloads, and BAV analysis.
- [Configuration](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/advanced/config/) - Information on using configuration files for consistent scanning settings.

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/JGPatelOfficial/xssfox) - Source code and issue tracking for the XSSFox project.
- [Community Resources](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/community/resources/) - Articles, videos, and tutorials from the XSSFox community.
- [API Documentation](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/modes/server-mode/#api-documentation) - Details on using XSSFox's REST API server mode.
- [MCP Integration](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/running/mcp/) - Guide to using XSSFox with AI assistants through Model Context Protocol.
- [GitHub Actions](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/running/github-action/) - Instructions for integrating XSSFox into CI/CD workflows.
- [Library Usage](https://xssfox.hahwul.com/page/running/code/) - Documentation on using XSSFox as a Go library in your own projects.
